name: Cottage Pie
prepNotes: Freeze the finished pie for up to 3 months. Reheat from frozen at 180°C for 45 minutes.
method: Peel and chop potatoes, boil until tender (20 min). Drain and mash with butter and milk. Season.
Fry onion in oil until soft. Add mince and brown all over.
Add tomato puree, Worcestershire sauce, diced carrots, peas and stock dissolved in 300ml water.
Simmer 20 minutes until sauce thickens. Season.
Transfer to oven dish, top with mash, rough up with a fork.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until golden. Rest 5 minutes before serving.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
prepNotes: Sauce freezes well for 3 months. Marinate chicken overnight for best flavour. Serve with rice or naan.

ingredients.name: tikka masala paste
ingredients.substitute: 2 tbsp curry powder + 1 tbsp garam masala

method: Mix chicken with yoghurt and half the tikka paste. Marinate 30 min (or overnight).
Grill or fry marinated chicken until charred and cooked through. Set aside.
Fry onion, garlic and ginger in oil until soft and golden.
Add remaining tikka paste and cook 2 min. Add tomatoes and simmer 10 min.
Stir in coconut cream and grilled chicken. Simmer 10 min.
Cook basmati rice according to packet. Serve curry over rice.

name: Spaghetti Bolognese
prepNotes: Bolognese sauce improves overnight. Freezes well for 3 months. Cook pasta fresh each day.
method: Finely dice onion, carrot and celery. Fry in oil 8 min until soft.
Add garlic, fry 1 min. Add mince and brown, breaking it up.
Stir in tomato puree, cook 2 min. Add tomatoes and crumbled stock cube.
Simmer on low heat 45 min, stirring occasionally. Season well.
Cook spaghetti per packet. Serve with sauce and grated Parmesan.

name: Chilli con Carne
prepNotes: Freezes brilliantly. Serve with rice, jacket potato or wraps. Great the next day.
method: Fry onion, pepper and garlic until soft.
Add mince and brown. Add spices and cook 2 min.
Add tomatoes and drained kidney beans. Stir in dark chocolate.
Simmer 40 min on low. Season. Cook rice and serve.

name: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl
prepNotes: Cook chicken and sauce fresh; rice can be batch-cooked and refrigerated 3 days.
method: Mix soy sauce, mirin, honey and garlic for the teriyaki sauce.
Fry chicken thighs skin-side down in sesame oil 6 min, flip and cook 4 min.
Pour in sauce, simmer until sticky and chicken is cooked through.
Cook rice per packet. Slice chicken over rice. Top with spring onions and sesame seeds.

name: Thai Green Chicken Curry
prepNotes: Best eaten fresh; coconut milk splits if frozen. Reheat gently. Leftovers keep 3 days.

ingredients.name: green curry paste
ingredients.substitute: 2 tbsp green curry powder + 1 tsp fish sauce

method: Fry curry paste in a wok 2 min until fragrant.
Add sliced chicken and stir-fry until sealed.
Pour in coconut milk and fish sauce. Simmer 10 min.
Add courgette and pepper. Cook 5 min. Stir in spinach.
Squeeze lime over. Serve with rice and fresh basil.

name: Chicken Souvlaki Bowl
prepNotes: Marinate chicken up to 24h. Tzatziki keeps 4 days in the fridge. Great for meal prep.
method: Marinate chicken in olive oil, lemon juice, garlic and oregano for 30 min.
Grill or griddle chicken 6-7 min each side. Rest and slice.
Grate cucumber, squeeze out moisture, mix with yoghurt, garlic and lemon for tzatziki.
Warm pitta. Serve chicken over pitta with tzatziki, tomatoes and red onion.

name: BBQ Pulled Pork Bowl
prepNotes: Slow cook 4h. Pulled pork freezes well for 3 months. Batch cook for excellent meal prep.
method: Rub pork with paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt and pepper.
Place in slow cooker with 100ml water. Cook on low 8h or high 4h.
Shred pork with two forks. Mix with BBQ sauce.
Combine coleslaw mix with mayo and vinegar. Season.
Serve pork over rice with slaw on the side.

name: Beef & Broccoli Stir Fry
prepNotes: Slice beef very thin and freeze 20 min for easier cutting. Best eaten fresh but keeps 2 days.
method: Toss sliced beef in cornflour. Mix soy, oyster sauce and 2 tbsp water.
Blanch broccoli in boiling water 2 min. Drain.
Stir-fry beef in very hot wok 2 min. Remove.
Fry garlic and ginger 30 sec. Add broccoli, then beef and sauce.
Toss everything 1 min. Serve over rice with spring onions.

name: Lamb Rogan Josh
prepNotes: Better the next day. Freezes perfectly for 3 months. Serve with rice or naan.
method: Cube lamb and brown in batches in hot oil. Set aside.
Fry onion until golden. Add garlic, ginger and rogan josh paste. Cook 3 min.
Return lamb, add tomatoes and 200ml water. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat, cover, simmer 75 min until lamb is tender.
Stir in yoghurt off heat. Season. Serve with rice.

name: Chicken Fajitas
prepNotes: Keep cooked chicken and peppers separate from wraps. Filling keeps 3 days in the fridge.

ingredients.name: fajita spice mix
ingredients.substitute: 1 tsp each: cumin, paprika, chilli, garlic powder

method: Slice chicken into strips and toss with fajita seasoning and oil.
Cook chicken in hot pan 6-8 min until cooked through. Remove.
Add sliced peppers and onion to the same pan. Stir-fry 5 min.
Warm tortillas. Fill with chicken, peppers, sour cream and cheese.
Squeeze lime over before serving.

name: Lemon Herb Salmon with Roasted Veg
prepNotes: Roast veg can be batch-cooked and stored 4 days. Season salmon fresh before cooking.
method: Halve potatoes, toss in olive oil and roast at 200°C for 15 min.
Add courgette and tomatoes. Roast further 10 min.
Place salmon on a baking tray. Season, top with lemon zest, dill and garlic.
Bake salmon at 200°C for 12-15 min. Serve with roasted veg.

name: Beef Ramen
prepNotes: Broth can be made ahead and stored 5 days. Cook noodles fresh per portion.

ingredients.name: miso paste
ingredients.substitute: 1 tbsp soy sauce + 1 tsp sesame oil

method: Soft boil eggs 7 min. Cool, peel and halve.
Sear beef in very hot pan 2 min per side for medium-rare. Rest and slice thin.
Combine stock, soy, miso, garlic and sesame oil. Bring to simmer.
Cook noodles per packet. Add baby corn to broth last 3 min.
Divide noodles into bowls. Ladle over broth with veg. Top with beef, egg and spring onions.

name: Chicken Pesto Pasta
prepNotes: Store pasta and sauce separately in the fridge for up to 3 days. Add a splash of pasta water when reheating.
method: Cook pasta per packet. Reserve a mug of pasta water before draining.
Season and fry chicken in olive oil until golden and cooked through. Slice.
In pasta pan, add pesto, tomatoes and spinach with splash of pasta water.
Toss in pasta and chicken. Top with Parmesan.

name: Thai Basil Pork Mince
prepNotes: Quick weeknight cook. Keep in fridge 3 days. Serve with fried egg on top.
method: Cook rice per packet.
Fry garlic and chilli in hot oil 30 sec.
Add pork mince, break up and fry on high until crispy.
Add fish sauce and oyster sauce. Stir. Add basil and toss.
Fry eggs sunny-side-up. Serve mince on rice topped with egg.

name: Smash Burger Bowl
prepNotes: Patties can be made and frozen raw. Cook direct from frozen adding 2 min per side.
method: Divide mince into 4 balls. Season.
Heat cast iron pan very hot. Place balls and smash flat with spatula.
Cook 2 min until crispy underneath. Flip, add cheese, cook 1 min.
Toast buns. Build: bun, lettuce, patty with cheese, tomato, gherkins, sauces.

name: Fish Pie
prepNotes: Assemble unbaked and freeze for up to 3 months. Add 15 min to baking from frozen.
method: Boil and mash potatoes with butter and milk. Season.
Poach fish in milk 8 min. Reserve the milk for the sauce.
Make white sauce: melt butter, add flour, gradually whisk in poaching milk.
Flake fish into an ovenproof dish. Add prawns and peas. Pour over sauce and dill.
Top with mash and grated cheese. Bake at 200°C for 25 min until golden.

name: Keema Curry
prepNotes: Freezes well. Serve with rice or warmed naan. Can be used in wraps for lunch.
method: Fry onion until golden. Add garlic and ginger, cook 2 min.
Add spices and cook 1 min. Add mince and brown.
Stir in tomatoes. Simmer 25 min.
Add peas in last 5 min. Season. Serve with rice.

name: Turkey Mince Chilli
prepNotes: Leaner than beef chilli. Freezes well. Great in wraps or over jacket potato.
method: Fry onion, pepper and garlic until soft.
Add turkey mince and brown.
Add spices and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and beans.
Simmer 30 min. Season. Serve with rice.

name: Greek Chicken Traybake
prepNotes: One-tray meal. Keeps 4 days in the fridge. Reheat in oven at 180°C for 15 min.
method: Toss potatoes in olive oil, oregano and salt. Spread in roasting tray. Roast 200°C for 15 min.
Add chicken thighs, tomatoes, red onion and olives to the tray.
Squeeze lemon over everything. Roast a further 30 min until chicken is golden.
Crumble feta over at the end. Serve straight from the tray.

name: Sausage Casserole
prepNotes: Freezes well. Serve with crusty bread or mash. Use good-quality pork sausages.
method: Brown sausages in an oven-proof pan. Remove.
Fry onion and garlic in same pan. Add paprika and thyme.
Add wine, let it bubble. Add tomatoes and beans.
Return sausages. Cover and bake at 180°C for 35 min.
Serve with crusty bread.

name: Char Siu Pork Bowl
prepNotes: Marinate pork overnight for best colour and flavour. Keeps 4 days sliced in the fridge.
method: Mix hoisin, honey, soy and five spice. Coat pork and marinate 1h (or overnight).
Roast pork at 200°C for 25 min, basting halfway, until caramelised.
Steam or stir-fry pak choi. Cook rice.
Slice pork. Serve over rice with pak choi, drizzle with any pan juices and sesame seeds.

name: Lamb Moussaka
prepNotes: Make ahead: assemble and refrigerate up to 2 days before baking. Freezes well.
method: Slice aubergine and potatoes. Brush with oil and roast at 200°C for 20 min.
Brown lamb with onion, garlic and cinnamon. Add tomatoes, simmer 20 min.
Make béchamel: melt butter, add flour, whisk in milk until thick. Season. Beat in eggs.
Layer: potato, lamb sauce, aubergine, repeat, top with béchamel and Parmesan.
Bake 180°C for 40 min until golden. Rest 10 min before serving.

name: Pad Thai with Chicken
prepNotes: Soak noodles in cold water rather than boiling for better texture. Best eaten fresh.

ingredients.name: tamarind paste
ingredients.substitute: 2 tbsp lime juice + 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce

method: Soak rice noodles in cold water 30 min, drain.
Mix fish sauce, tamarind and 2 tbsp sugar for the sauce.
Stir-fry chicken in very hot wok until cooked. Push to one side, scramble eggs.
Add noodles, sauce and beansprouts. Toss 2 min.
Serve topped with spring onions, crushed peanuts and lime wedges.

name: Chicken Carbonara
prepNotes: Make the egg sauce off the heat to avoid scrambling. Best eaten fresh.
method: Cook spaghetti per packet. Reserve a large mug of pasta water.
Whisk eggs with grated Parmesan and black pepper.
Fry lardons and garlic until crispy. Season and fry chicken until golden. Slice.
Off the heat, add drained pasta and egg mixture with a splash of pasta water.
Toss until creamy. Add chicken. Serve immediately with extra Parmesan.

name: Beef Tacos
prepNotes: Cook mince ahead and keep in the fridge 4 days. Assemble tacos fresh each time.

ingredients.name: taco seasoning
ingredients.substitute: 1 tsp each: cumin, chilli powder, garlic powder, paprika

method: Brown beef mince. Add taco seasoning and 100ml water. Simmer 5 min.
Dice tomatoes and red onion. Mash avocado with lime juice.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan.
Build tacos: tortilla, mince, guacamole, tomato, cheese, sour cream.

name: Chicken Burrito Bowl
prepNotes: Store components separately and assemble fresh. All components keep 4 days.
method: Season chicken with fajita spice and grill or fry until cooked through. Slice.
Cook rice with lime zest and fresh coriander stirred through.
Warm black beans in a pan with a pinch of cumin.
Stir-fry peppers until slightly charred.
Build bowl: rice, beans, chicken, peppers, lettuce, cheese, sour cream.

name: Smoked Haddock Kedgeree
prepNotes: Keep rice and fish separate if storing. Reheat gently with a splash of water. Keeps 2 days.
method: Poach haddock in milk 8-10 min. Flake, discard skin.
Hard boil eggs 9 min. Peel and quarter.
Fry onion in butter until golden. Add curry powder, cook 2 min.
Add rice to pan. Cook per packet with the poaching milk topped up with water.
Fold in peas, flaked fish and eggs. Top with parsley and lemon.

name: Lamb Chops with Mint Sauce and Greens
prepNotes: Best cooked fresh. Marinate up to 2h for extra flavour. Pair with whatever greens you have.
method: Boil baby potatoes until tender.
Make mint sauce: finely chop mint, mix with vinegar, pinch of sugar and salt.
Rub lamb chops with garlic and olive oil. Season.
Griddle or pan-fry chops 3-4 min per side for medium. Rest.
Steam broccoli. Serve chops with potatoes, broccoli and mint sauce.

name: Chicken & Mushroom Risotto
prepNotes: Risotto does not freeze well. Store in fridge 2 days and reheat with a splash of stock.
method: Fry chicken until golden. Slice and set aside.
Fry onion and garlic until soft. Add mushrooms and cook until moisture evaporates.
Add rice, stir to coat. Add wine, let it absorb.
Add stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly, 20-25 min until creamy and tender.
Stir in butter and Parmesan. Add sliced chicken. Season and serve.

name: Gyudon (Japanese Beef Bowl)
prepNotes: The beef topping keeps 4 days in the fridge. Cook rice fresh per portion.
method: Thinly slice beef (partially freeze for easier slicing).
Simmer dashi, soy and mirin in pan. Add sliced onion and cook 5 min.
Add beef, cook 3-4 min until just cooked.
Cook rice. Serve beef and onions over rice with a raw or soft-boiled egg on top.
Garnish with spring onions and pickled ginger.

name: Turkey Stuffed Peppers
prepNotes: Can be assembled ahead and refrigerated unbaked for up to 24h. Freezes well.
method: Par-cook rice 10 min. Drain.
Halve peppers and remove seeds. Place cut-side up in roasting tray.
Fry onion, garlic and mince. Add tomatoes, oregano, paprika. Simmer 10 min.
Mix rice into sauce. Fill peppers with mixture. Top with mozzarella.
Bake at 190°C for 30 min until peppers are tender and cheese is golden.

name: Baked Cod with Chorizo & Chickpeas
prepNotes: Cook fish fresh — it does not keep well. The chickpea base can be made 2 days ahead.
method: Fry chorizo until crispy. Add garlic and paprika.
Add chickpeas and tomatoes. Simmer 10 min. Wilt in spinach.
Place in ovenproof dish. Season cod fillets and lay on top.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon. Bake at 200°C for 15 min.

name: Thai Beef Salad
prepNotes: Dress salad just before serving. Beef can be marinated and refrigerated for up to 24h.
method: Sear beef in very hot pan 2-3 min per side. Rest 5 min. Slice thin.
Mix fish sauce, lime juice and chilli for dressing.
Combine leaves, tomatoes, cucumber and red onion.
Toss with dressing and beef. Top with coriander and mint.

name: Scotch Egg Protein Bowl
prepNotes: Scotch eggs keep 4 days in the fridge. Great for on-the-go lunches. Assemble bowl fresh.
method: Soft boil 4 eggs 6 min. Cool in ice water, peel.
Remove sausage meat from skins. Mix with mustard.
Wrap each egg in sausage meat. Coat in flour, beaten egg, then breadcrumbs.
Bake at 200°C for 25 min until golden.
Serve halved over salad leaves and cherry tomatoes.

name: Sticky Chicken Wings with Slaw
prepNotes: Wings can be marinated overnight. Cook fresh — they lose crispness when reheated.
method: Toss wings in garlic powder, paprika, salt. Bake at 220°C for 40 min, turning halfway.
Mix BBQ sauce, honey and soy. Toss cooked wings in sauce.
Return to oven 5-10 min until sticky and caramelised.
Mix coleslaw with mayo and vinegar. Serve wings with slaw.

name: Chinese Lemon Chicken
prepNotes: Sauce keeps 3 days. Fry chicken fresh per portion for best texture.
method: Cut chicken into chunks, coat in cornflour, salt and pepper.
Shallow fry chicken until golden and crispy. Drain.
Make sauce: lemon juice and zest, honey, soy, garlic, 1 tsp cornflour with 100ml water. Simmer until thick.
Toss chicken in sauce. Serve over rice with spring onions.

name: Salmon Don
prepNotes: Salmon is best cooked fresh. Sushi rice can be made ahead and kept covered at room temperature up to 4h.
method: Cook sushi rice per packet. Season with rice vinegar, sugar and salt.
Pan-fry salmon skin-side down 4 min, flip 2 min. Flake.
Slice avocado and cucumber.
Serve rice in bowls topped with salmon, avocado, cucumber, pickled ginger and sesame seeds.
Drizzle soy sauce over.

name: Protein Chocolate Brownies
prepNotes: Store in an airtight container for up to 5 days. Freeze individual squares up to 3 months.
method: Preheat oven to 180°C. Line a 20cm square tin.
Mix eggs, almond butter and honey until smooth.
Stir in protein powder, cocoa and baking powder.
Fold in chocolate chips.
Pour into tin and bake 20-25 min. Cool before cutting into 8 squares.

name: Greek Yoghurt Berry Parfait
prepNotes: Assemble fresh for best texture. All components keep separately for the week.
method: Defrost berries or use fresh.
Layer yoghurt, berries and granola in a glass or bowl.
Drizzle with honey. Serve immediately.

name: Dark Chocolate Mousse
prepNotes: Make the day before — improves overnight in the fridge. Keeps 3 days.
method: Melt chocolate gently over a bain-marie. Cool slightly.
Separate eggs. Whisk yolks with honey, fold into chocolate.
Whisk egg whites to stiff peaks. Fold into chocolate mixture in thirds.
Fold in Greek yoghurt. Spoon into glasses, chill 2h minimum.

name: Banana Nice Cream
prepNotes: Freeze bananas overnight. Blend direct from frozen. Can be refrozen for up to 2 weeks.
method: Peel and slice bananas. Freeze overnight.
Blend frozen banana with protein powder and peanut butter until creamy.
Eat immediately as soft-serve or refreeze 1h for firmer texture.

name: Oat & Honey Protein Cookies
prepNotes: Store in an airtight container for up to 1 week. Freeze up to 3 months.
method: Preheat oven to 170°C. Line a baking tray.
Mix all ingredients together until combined.
Roll into 12 balls, flatten slightly on tray.
Bake 12-15 min until golden. Cool on tray — they firm as they cool.

name: Vanilla Chia Pudding
prepNotes: Make 4 portions at once, they keep in the fridge 5 days. Stir before serving.
method: Whisk milk, protein powder and honey together.
Stir in chia seeds. Divide into 4 jars.
Refrigerate overnight (minimum 4h), stirring after 1h.
Top with berries before serving.

name: Creamy Rice Pudding
prepNotes: Keeps 4 days in the fridge. Reheat with a splash of milk. Serve warm or cold.
method: Bring milk to a gentle simmer with vanilla and nutmeg.
Add rice and stir. Cook on low heat 35-40 min, stirring often, until creamy.
Stir in honey off the heat. Add yoghurt for extra creaminess.
Serve warm with a drizzle of honey.

name: Baked Cinnamon Apple with Yoghurt
prepNotes: Apples can be stuffed ahead and refrigerated. Bake fresh when needed.
method: Core apples without going all the way through.
Mix oats, honey, cinnamon and butter. Press into apple cavities.
Bake at 180°C for 25 min until apples are tender.
Serve warm with a dollop of Greek yoghurt.
